name: pysweepme

metadata.name: pysweepme
metadata.summary: Load SweepMe! instrument drivers in your own python projects.

documentation:
# pysweepme

[SweepMe!](https://sweep-me.net) is a program to create measurement procedures in short time. The communication with the
instruments is handled via instrument drivers ("Device Classes"), that are python based code snippets. To use these drivers in independent python projects, you can use pysweepme to load them including the creation of interface ports. The package pysweepme outsources parts of SweepMe! as open source MIT licensed code to allow loading drivers in your own scripts.

## Installation
So far, only Windows is supported. Other systems might work as well but probably some modifications are needed.
We recommend to use Python 3.9 64bit, as this is the python version and architecture targeted by us. In addition,
several drivers in our [instrument driver repository](https://github.com/SweepMe/instrument-drivers) bring their own
dependencies which are bundled for Python 3.9.

Use the command line (cmd) to install/uninstall:

### install
    pip install pysweepme 

### install with force version
    pip install pysweepme==1.5.6.10

### uninstall
    pip uninstall pysweepme
    
### upgrade
    pip install pysweepme --upgrade

## Usage

1. Copy the drivers to a certain folder in your project folder, e.g "Devices" or to the public folder "CustomDevices".
2. Import pysweepme to your project.
3. Use 'get_driver' to load a driver.
4. See the source code of the driver to see which commands are available.  
   A general overview of the semantic functions of a driver can be found in the [SweepMe! wiki](https://wiki.sweep-me.net/wiki/Sequencer_procedure).

## Example

```python

import pysweepme

# find a certain folder that is used by SweepMe!
custom_devices_folder = pysweepme.get_path("CUSTOMDEVICES")

# folder is a path from which instrument drivers will be loaded
# port is a string, e.g. "COM1" or "GPIB0::24::INSTR"
mouse = pysweepme.get_driver("Logger-PC_Mouse", folder=".", port_string="")
mouse.connect()

print(mouse.read())
```
    
## Version number
The version number of pysweepme correlates with the version number of SweepMe!. For example, pysweepme 1.5.5.x is
related to SweepMe! 1.5.5.x, but the last digit of the version number can differ.

## Source code
The source code can be found on github.

## Instrument drivers
* Instrument drivers might depend on further python packages that are part of SweepMe! but are not shipped with 
pysweepme. In this case, these packages have to be installed using pip by solving the ImportErrors. 
* Some Instrument drivers only work with Windows and will not work with other systems, e.g. due to dll files or certain 
third-party packages.
* Instrument drivers can be downloaded from https://sweep-me.net/devices or using the version manager in SweepMe!.
  Developers can also find the source code of the drivers in our [instrument driver repository on github](https://github.com/SweepMe/instrument-drivers).
* SweepMe! instrument drivers have two purposes. They have semantic standard function to be used in SweepMe! but also 
wrap communication commands to easily call them with pysweepme. Not all SweepMe! instrument drivers come with wrapped
communication commands, yet.

## Changelog
You can find the list of changes on the [releases page on github](https://github.com/SweepMe/pysweepme/releases).

The changelog for pysweepme 1.5.5 is still available in the [README of the 1.5.5 branch](https://github.com/SweepMe/pysweepme/blob/v1.5.5.x/README.md#changelog).

functions.name: pysweepme.DeviceManager.get_main_py_path
documentation:
Find the main python file matching the current architecture best.

In a given folder, look for the main<suffix>.py file that matches the running python version and bitness.
Return the generic main.py path if the specific one does not exist.

Args:
    path: Path to the folder that contains the main<suffix>.py file

Returns:
    The path to the main<suffix>.py file.

functions.name: pysweepme.DeviceManager.get_driver_module
documentation:
Load the module containing the requested driver.

Args:
    folder: The folder containing the drivers.
    name: The name of the driver

Returns:
    The loaded module containing the requested driver.

functions.name: pysweepme.DeviceManager.get_driver_class
documentation:
Get the class (not an instance) of the requested driver.

Args:
    folder: The folder containing the drivers.
    name: The name of the driver

Returns:
    The class of the requested driver.

functions.name: pysweepme.DeviceManager.get_driver_instance
documentation:
Create a bare driver instance.

Create a bare driver instance without input cleanup and without setting GUI parameters.

Args:
    folder: General folder in which to look for drivers.
    name: Name of the driver being the name of the driver folder.

Returns:
    Device object of the driver.

functions.name: pysweepme.DeviceManager.setup_driver
documentation:
Set port and device.

The GUI parameters Device and Port (if provided) are set.
When using the Port Manager, the port object is attached to the driver.

Args:
    driver: The driver instance.
    name: The name of the driver.
    port_string: The string defining the port to use for the driver.

functions.name: pysweepme.DeviceManager.get_driver
documentation:
Create a driver instance.

When the driver uses the port manager, the port will already be opened, but the connect() function of
the driver must be called in any case.

Args:
    name: Name of the driver being the name of the driver folder
    folder: (optional) General folder to look for drivers, If folder is not used or empty, the driver is loaded
        from the folder of the running script/project
    port_string: (optional) A port resource name as selected in SweepMe! such as 'COM1', 'GPIB0::1::INSTR', etc.
        It is required if the driver connects to an instrument and needs to open a specific port.

Returns:
    Initialized Device object of the driver with port and default parameters set.

classes.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.register_get_input
documentation:
Register a handler for the get_input() function.

Args:
    get_input_function: Function that shall handle calls to get_input().

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.get_input
documentation:
Ask the user for input.

This function can be used by drivers or CustomFunction scripts to ask the user for input. If used with SweepMe!,
this function will be overwritten by SweepMe! to create a graphical user interface.

Args:
    msg: String of the message that is displayed to the user.

Returns:
    str: response message

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.register_message_box
documentation:
Register a handler for the message_box() function.

Args:
    message_box_function: Function that shall handle calls to message_box().

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.message_box
documentation:
Show a message to the user.

This function can be used by drivers or CustomFunction scripts to show a message to the user.
If used with SweepMe!, this function will be overwritten by SweepMe! to create a graphical message box.
By default, it will be just printed to the console.

Args:
    msg: String of the message that is displayed to the user.
    blocking: True to require the user to acknowledge the message.

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.register_message_info
documentation:
Register a handler for the message_info() function.

Args:
    message_info_function: Function that shall handle calls to message_info().

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.message_info
documentation:
Show an info to the user.

This function can be used by drivers or CustomFunction scripts to show a message to the user.
If used with SweepMe!, this function will be overwritten by SweepMe! to add an entry in the general tab
of the main window. By default, it will be just printed to the console.

Args:
    msg: String of the message that is displayed to the user.

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.register_message_balloon
documentation:
Register a handler for the message_balloon() function.

Args:
    message_balloon_function: Function that shall handle calls to message_balloon().

methods.name: pysweepme.UserInterface.UIHandler.message_balloon
documentation:
Show a message balloon to the user.

This function can be used by drivers or CustomFunction scripts to show a message to the user.
If used with SweepMe!, this function will be overwritten by SweepMe! to create a message balloon in the
system tray. By default, it will be just printed to the console.

Args:
    msg: String of the message that is displayed to the user.
Manages calls to user interface functions and allows applications to register alternative functions.

functions.name: pysweepme.ErrorMessage.try_to_print_traceback
documentation:
Print a traceback for the most recent python exception.

If there is a UnicodeDecodeError while trying to print the exception, e.g.
because a Python source file containing an error is using non UTF-8 encoding,
the function tries to monkey patch the traceback module to skip reading lines
from the file and still try to print as many error details as possible.

functions.name: pysweepme.ErrorMessage.error
documentation:
Print arguments to the debug log including an exception stacktrace.

Args:
    *args: The arguments to print to the debug log.

functions.name: pysweepme.ErrorMessage.debug
documentation:
Print arguments to the debug log.

Args:
    *args: The arguments to print to the debug log.
    debugmode_only: True if the arguments shall be printed only when debug mode is on.

functions.name: pysweepme.ErrorMessage.debug_only
documentation:
Print arguments to the debug log if debug mode is on.

Args:
    *args: The arguments to print to the debug log.

functions.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.addFolderToPATH
documentation:
Add libraries folder of calling script to the PATH.

Used by DeviceClasses and CustomFunctions to add their path to PATH.
If no argument is given, the path of the calling file is used.

functions.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.addModuleFolderToPATH
documentation:
Add libraries folder of calling module to the PATH.

Used by Modules to add their path to PATH.
If no argument is given, the path of the calling Module is used.

functions.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.get_path
documentation: returns a path for a given identifier, such as 'CUSTOMDEVICES', 'DEVICES', ...

functions.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.set_path
documentation: sets a path for a given identifier, such as 'CUSTOMDEVICES', 'DEVICES', ...

functions.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.main_is_frozen
documentation: remains for compatibility

functions.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.getFoMa
documentation: The FolderManager is already required within pysweepme itself, or even the FolderManager module.
But we do not want to initialize the FolderManager already when importing, but only when it is used.
This function is mainly for pysweepme itself, as application code which is using pysweepme can also
use FolderManager() directly (which is a singleton).

classes.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.FolderManagerInstance

methods.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.FolderManagerInstance.__init__
documentation: create defines whether folders are created. When used with pysweepme, the default will not create folders
This class allows to create multiple instances of the folder manager. This is only required in
rare cases, like getting a folder manager instance that corresponds to the folders of a different
process with another instance_id and other paths for the writable objects.

classes.name: pysweepme.FolderManager.FolderManager
documentation: Singleton Wrapper around the FolderManager, as one usually only wants to use one instance of the FolderManager
throughout the whole application.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.device_communication
documentation: Single (global) dictionary where drivers can store their information that can be shared across instances.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.clear_device_communication
documentation: Clear all information that have been stored in the device_communication dictionary.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.list_functions
documentation:
Returns a list of all function names that are individually defined by the driver, e.g. get/set functions.

These functions can be be used in python projects when using pysweepme to directly access instrument properties.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.store_parameter
documentation:
Stores a value in the ParameterStore for a given key.

Drivers can use the ParameterStore to store information and restore the same information later even in
a new instance.

Args:
    key: The key under which the information is stored. It should be unique and not conflicting with
         other drivers.
    value: The information to be stored.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.restore_parameter
documentation:
Restores a parameter from the ParameterStore for a given key.

Args:
    key: The key under which the information was stored before.

Returns:
    The stored information, or None if no information can be found under the given key.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice._on_run
documentation: Called by SweepMe! at the beginning of the run to indicate the start. Do not overwrite it.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice._on_stop
documentation: Called by SweepMe! in case the user requests a stop of the run. Do not overwrite it.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.is_run_stopped
documentation:
This function can be used in a driver to figure out whether a stop of the run was requested by the user
It is helpful if a driver function is caught in a while-loop.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_Folder
documentation: Easy access to a folder without the need to import the FolderManager.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_folder
documentation: Easy access to a folder without the need to import the FolderManager.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.isConfigFile
documentation: deprecated: remains for compatibility reasons.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.is_configfile
documentation: This function checks whether a driver related config file exists.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.getConfigSections
documentation: deprecated: remains for compatibility reasons.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_configsections
documentation:
This function returns all sections of the driver related config file.

If not file exists, an empty list is returned.

Returns:
    List of Strings

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.getConfigOptions
documentation: deprecated: remains for compatibility reasons.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_configoptions
documentation:
This functions returns all key-value options of a given section of the driver related config file.

If the file does not exist, an empty dictionary is returned.

Args:
    section: str, a config file section

Returns:
    dict with pairs of key-value options

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.getConfig
documentation: deprecated: remains for compatibility reasons.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_config
documentation:
This function returns a representation of the driver related config file by means of a nested dictionary
that contains for each section a dictionary with the options.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_GUIparameter
documentation: Is overwritten by Device Class to retrieve the GUI parameter selected by the user.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.set_GUIparameter
documentation: Is overwritten by Device Class to set the GUI parameter a user can select.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.reset_latest_parameters
documentation:
Initialize or reset the saved parameters to their default.

The parameters will be set to their default values. If Port / Device were already set, these properties
are retained.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.set_parameters
documentation:
Overwrite GUI parameters.

Args:
    parameters: Dictionary mapping from keys-to-overwrite to the new values.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_parameters
documentation:
Retrieve the parameters that are currently saved for the device.

Returns:
    Mapping of parameter keys to their current values.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.connect
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed and not using the port manager.
# can be used by device class to be triggered by button find_Ports
def find_Ports(self):
# not needed anymore here
# the module checks whether the functions exists
def get_CalibrationFile_properties(self, port = ""):

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.disconnect
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.initialize
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.deinitialize
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.reconfigure
documentation:
Function to be overridden if needed.

if a GUI parameter changes after replacement with global parameters, the device needs to be reconfigure.
Default behavior is that all parameters are set again and 'configure' is called.
The device class maintainer can redefine/overwrite 'reconfigure' with a more individual procedure.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.configure
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.unconfigure
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.poweron
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.poweroff
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.signin
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.signout
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice._transfer
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.start
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.apply
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.reach
documentation:
Actively wait until the applied value is reached.

Optional, can be overriden by the device class if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.adapt
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.adapt_ready
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.trigger_ready
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.measure
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.request_result
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.read_result
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.process_data
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.call
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.finish
documentation: Function to be overridden if needed.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.stop_Measurement
documentation:
deprecated: use 'raise Exception(...)' instead
sets flag to stop a measurement, not supported in pysweepme standalone.
def set_Parameter(self,feature,value): # not used yet
pass
def get_Parameter(self,feature): # not used yet
pass

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.stop_measurement
documentation:
deprecated: use 'raise Exception(...)' instead
sets flag to stop a measurement, not supported in pysweepme standalone.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.write_Log
documentation: deprecated, remains for compatibility reasons.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.write_log
documentation: deprecated, remains for compatibility reasons.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.message_log
documentation: Writes message to logbook file.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.message_Info
documentation: Command is deprecated, use 'message_info' instead.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.message_info
documentation: Write to info box.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.message_Box
documentation: Command is deprecated, use 'message_box' instead.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.message_box
documentation: Creates a message box with given message.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.message_balloon
documentation: Creates a message balloon with given message.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_variables
documentation: Returns a list of strings being the variable of the Device Class.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.get_units
documentation: Returns a list of strings being the units of the Device Class.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.apply_value
documentation: Convenience function for user to apply a value, mainly for use with pysweepme.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.write
documentation: Applies and reaches the given value as new sweep value for the selected SweepMode.

methods.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.read
documentation:
returns a list of values according to functions 'get_variables' and 'get_units'
convenience function for pysweepme to quickly retrieve values by calling several semantic standard functions.

class_variables.name: pysweepme.EmptyDeviceClass.EmptyDevice.actions
documentation: static variable that can be used in a driver to define a list of function names that can be used as action

classes.name: pysweepme.Config.DefaultFileIO

methods.name: pysweepme.Config.DefaultFileIO.register_custom_default
documentation:
Register an alternative default for creating a Path-compatible instance for file IO operations.

Args:
    function: Function that accepts a Path or string representing the file and returns an instance that behaves
              like pathlib.Path()

methods.name: pysweepme.Config.DefaultFileIO.pysweepme_default_fileio
documentation:
Create a pathlib.Path instance.

Args:
    file: Path or string representing the file.

Returns:
    Path instance for the requested argument.

methods.name: pysweepme.Config.DefaultFileIO.default_fileio
documentation:
Create a pathlib.Path() compatible instance for the requested file.

If the application did not register an alternative default, a pathlib.Path() instance will be returned.

Args:
    file: Path or string representing the file.

Returns: An instance that behaves like pathlib.Path()

Manages which class shall be used to perform File IO operations.

By default, pathlib.Path() will be used, but the application may register an alternative default that behaves
the same when calling open() in a `with` statement.

classes.name: pysweepme.Config.Config

methods.name: pysweepme.Config.Config.__init__
documentation:
Create a Config instance.

The Config instance should always be created directly before it is used, to make use of the incremental write
capabilities that reduce conflicts when using multiple instances of the application. That way, the Config class
will read the current config ini file, apply the requested changes only to the respective section and key, and
write those changes without overwriting other sections/keys that might have been modified by another program.

Args:
    file_name: The path (Path object or string) to the config file.
               Deprecated: A string containing the contents of an ini file.
    custom_reader_writer: If this argument is not provided (recommended), the file operations will be performed
                          using python's pathlib.Path() (unless the application registered another default).
                          This argument can be a function returning another instance of a pathlib.Path()
                          compatible class that shall be used for file IO operations only for this config
                          instance.

documentation: Function to return a file descriptor.

documentation: Function to close context manager.
Protocol for a ContextManager for IO Operations.

classes.name: pysweepme.pysweepme_types.FileIOProtocolWithoutModifiedCheck

methods.name: pysweepme.pysweepme_types.FileIOProtocolWithoutModifiedCheck.open
documentation: Function to open a file compatible with pathlib.Path when used in a context manager.

Protocol for a class with a pathlib.Path compatible open() function that returns a context manager.

In contrast to Path's open(), this function does not return a file descriptor directly and instead always
must be used in conjunction with a `with` statement that will return the file descriptor of the opened file.

classes.name: pysweepme.pysweepme_types.FileIOProtocolWithModifiedCheck

methods.name: pysweepme.pysweepme_types.FileIOProtocolWithModifiedCheck.set_full_read
documentation:
Function that shall be called when a file is read completely.

If a file was modified by an external program, the user does not need to decide whether to overwrite the file
or not, if the respective file was only read after the external modification.

Protocol for a class with a pathlib.Path compatible open() function that returns a context manager.

In contrast to Path's open(), this function does not return a file descriptor directly and instead always
must be used in conjunction with a `with` statement that will return the file descriptor of the opened file.

returns a list of resources for given port types
Attention: port identification is not properly implemented. Only works for USBTMC and if identification was
already retrieved beforehand

Args:
    port_types: list of port types
    port_identification: list of identification strings

Returns:
    List of resource strings

returns a pysweepme Port object that is opened

Returns:
    pysweepme Port object

methods.name: pysweepme.Ports.COMport.get_identification
documentation:
Get details of the COM port.

In contrast to the other get_identification functions which return instrument identifications, this
function returns details about the COM Port (or USB-COM adapter if one is used) itself.
Therefore this should be considered an unstable feature that might be changed in the future.

Returns:
    The hwid of the COM port.
